original_abstract:
### Background
Chatbots are sophisticated computer programs that leverage artificial intelligence (AI) and natural language processing (NLP) to simulate human-like conversations. Among these technologies, ChatGPT—driven by OpenAI’s Generative Pre-trained Transformer (GPT) architecture—has gained significant attention for its remarkable capacity to generate coherent prose. However, its deployment in academic and technical contexts has sparked debate regarding its susceptibility to "hallucinations," which can result in factual inaccuracies and the fabrication of bibliographic references. This study evaluates the reliability of generative AI by investigating the frequency and nature of such hallucinations within research proposals drafted entirely by ChatGPT.
humanized_abstract:
### Background
Chatbots are computer programs that use artificial intelligence (AI) and natural language processing (NLP) to imitate human conversations. Utilizing a ChatGPT technology developed from OpenAI’s Generative Pre-trained Transformer (GPT) language model, this study examined the effectiveness and reliability of such technology in academic and technical applications and subsequently identifying sources of potential errors, specifically hallucinations that can cause inaccuracies in the statements and fabrication of the reference list entries. The purpose of this study was to determine the reliability of generative AI, and investigated the occurrence and nature of errors that occurred in the research proposals generated with the assistance of ChatGPT.

document.input:
### Background
Chatbots are sophisticated computer programs that leverage artificial intelligence (AI) and natural language processing (NLP) to simulate human-like conversations. Among these technologies, ChatGPT—driven by OpenAI’s Generative Pre-trained Transformer (GPT) architecture—has gained significant attention for its remarkable capacity to generate coherent prose. However, its deployment in academic and technical contexts has sparked debate regarding its susceptibility to "hallucinations," which can result in factual inaccuracies and the fabrication of bibliographic references. This study evaluates the reliability of generative AI by investigating the frequency and nature of such hallucinations within research proposals drafted entirely by ChatGPT.
